sections.paragraph:
λόγιον, ου, τό (Eur., Hdt.+, mostly of short divine sayings: Hdt. 8, 60, 3; Thu. 2, 8, 2; Polyb. 3, 112, 8; 8, 30, 6; Diod S 2, 14, 3; 2, 26, 9; 4, 65, 3 al.; Aelian, VH 2, 41. Likew. LXX [TManson, Goguel Festschr. ’50, 142f]; TestLevi 8:2; TestBenj 9:1 v.l.; EpArist 177; Philo, Congr. Erud. Grat. 134, Fuga 60, Mos. 2, 262, Praem. 1, Vi. Cont. 25; Jos., Bell. 6, 311; Just.) a saying, in our lit. only pl. (as also predom. in other wr.); of the revelations received by Moses λόγια ζῶντα Ac 7:38. Of God’s promises to the Jews Ro 3:2 (JDoeve, Studia Paulina [JdeZwaan Festschr.] ’53, 111–23). Of words fr. Scripture gener. (as Plut., Fab. 4, 5 of words fr. the Sibylline books; contrast the sg. of a biblical statement: Just., A I, 32, 14 κατὰ τὸ λόγιον [Is 11:1]; Did., Gen. 122, 24 [Eccl 8:1]); τὰ λόγια τοῦ θεοῦ (LXX; Iren. 1, 8, 1 [Harv. I 68, 11]; cp. τὰ θεῖα λ. Did., Gen. 174, 16) Hb 5:12.—1 Cl 13:4; 19:1; 53:1. τὰ λ. τῆς παιδείας τοῦ θεοῦ the oracles of God’s teaching 62:3 (cp. τὰ τῆς τοῦ θεοῦ σοφίας λ. Orig., C. Cels. 5, 29, 50). Also of NT sayings 2 Cl 13:3 (cp. vs. 4). Likew. τὰ λόγια τοῦ κυρίου the sayings of the Lord (Jesus; Marinus, Vi. Procli 26 p. 163, 50 Boiss. τὰ Ὀρφέως λόγια; TestBenj 9:1 v.l. ἀπὸ λογίων Ἑνώχ) Pol 7:1; cp. Papias (2:15f; in Eus., HE 3, 39, 1); AcPlCor 2:3 (Clem. Al., Quis Div. Salv. 3, 1; Iren. 1, Praef. 1 [Harv. I 2, 4]; cp. Just., D. 18, 1). Of the sayings of Christians who have been endowed with the gift of ministry through spoken words 1 Pt 4:11. For Papias s. also ἐξήγησις and κυριακός.—PNepper-Christensen, Mt ein Judenchristliches Evangelium? ’58, 37–56.—DELG s.v. λέγω B 2. M-M. TW.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. pl. = plural

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

  1. wr. = writers, writings
  2. fr. = from

gener. gener. = generally

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. sg. = singular

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

NT NT = New Testament

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Marinus, Vi. Procli Marinus, Vi. Procli = Marinus the phil., Vita Procli; V a.d.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

Clem. Clem. Al. = Clement of Alexandria, II–III a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 598
